steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes two subtypes of austenitic stainless steel: the 300 series (aerospace tubing) and the 200 series (home water tanks), noting that the 200 series has less nickel and more manganese than the 300 series. The blank must complete a logical inference about aerospace tubing vs. home water tanks.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — aerospace tubing will have similar magnetic properties to stainless steel used to manufacture induction cookers.: Magnetic properties belong to ferritic stainless steel; austenitic steel (including the 300 series used for aerospace) is not magnetic. This conflates ferritic and austenitic. ✗
  • Choice B — both aerospace tubing and home water tanks will have a face-centered cubic crystalline structure, but stainless steel used to manufacture aerospace tubing will have less nickel than stainless steel used to manufacture home water tanks will: The first clause is true (both are austenitic), but the second reverses the nickel relationship. The 300 series has more nickel than the 200 series, so aerospace tubing has more nickel than home water tanks, not less. ✗
  • Choice C — aerospace tubing will have a concentration of manganese greater than 10.5%, while stainless steel used to manufacture home water tanks will not.: The 10.5% threshold is the minimum chromium requirement for all stainless steel — it says nothing about manganese percentages. ✗
  • Choice D — aerospace tubing will have less manganese in its composition than stainless steel used to manufacture home water tanks will.: The 300 series (aerospace) has less manganese than the 200 series (home water tanks). This follows directly from the passage. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
